strings.app_title: CoffeeVision: AI-based Coffee leaf disease detector and Advisory system
strings.app_subtitle: Sign in to classify coffee leaf images and view disease advisories
strings.upload_title: Upload or Capture Coffee Leaf Image
strings.chat_subtitle: Offline chat about coffee farming and leaf diseases
strings.chat_placeholder: Ask about diseases, treatment, or farming...
strings.chat_empty_hint: Ask anything about coffee leaf health or farming.
strings.error_not_leaf:
⚠️ This image is not a coffee leaf.
Please upload a valid coffee leaf image.
strings.about_p1: Our Coffee Leaf Disease Detection System leverages Artificial Intelligence to help farmers identify and classify coffee leaf diseases such as Leaf Rust and Phoma early. This empowers farmers to take timely preventive measures, reducing crop losses and improving yields.
strings.about_p2: The project aims to support sustainable coffee farming in Uganda and promote digital agriculture solutions.
strings.about_p3: Developed by Joseph Walusimbi and Chelangat Specioza, passionate data analysts and AI enthusiasts focused on applying machine learning to solve real-world challenges in agriculture and climate.
strings.team_intro: Electronics and Computer Engineering team from Soroti University, Uganda — building intelligent solutions for agriculture and rural communities.
strings.specioza_contrib_1: Sourced and prepared the coffee leaf disease dataset
strings.specioza_contrib_2: Trained the DenseNet121 model to classify healthy leaves, Leaf rust, and Phoma
strings.specioza_contrib_3: Built the first web interface: upload or capture a leaf photo and get a classification result
strings.specioza_contrib_4: Delivered the original English-only disease detection workflow
strings.joseph_contrib_1: Integrated the offline GGUF language model (SmolLM2-360M) via llama.cpp
strings.joseph_contrib_2: Designed and built the Ask CoffeeVision farmer chatbot
strings.joseph_contrib_3: Added bilingual support — English and Kiswahili across the UI, advisories, and chat
strings.joseph_contrib_4: Extended the system for ADTC 2026: offline architecture, metadata, profiler, and documentation

advisories.healthy_leaves:
✅ Healthy Leaf Detected!

Best practices to keep your coffee healthy:
• Check leaves weekly for yellow spots, dark lesions, or unusual leaf drop
• Keep good spacing between plants and prune for airflow
• Use balanced fertilizer (nitrogen and potassium)
• Remove fallen leaves and keep the field clean
• Avoid overhead irrigation when possible
• Choose disease-resistant coffee varieties where available
• Watch closely during rainy seasons
advisories.leaf_rust:
☕ Leaf Rust Detected!

Symptoms:
• Yellow-orange powdery spots on the underside of leaves
• Premature leaf drop

Recommended Countermeasures:
• Apply copper-based or systemic fungicides (e.g., triazoles, strobilurins) at early stages
• Remove, prune, and safely destroy infected leaves to prevent spread
• Reduce plant overcrowding to improve airflow and lower humidity
• Apply balanced fertilizers (especially nitrogen and potassium) to boost plant immunity
• Use rust-resistant coffee varieties where possible
• Monitor frequently during wet seasons when rust spreads fastest
advisories.phoma:
Phoma Detected!

Symptoms:
• Dark brown to black lesions on leaf margins
• Yellow halos surrounding infected spots
• Premature leaf drop, especially in young plants

Recommended Countermeasures:
• Remove and destroy infected leaves to reduce fungal spread
• Apply appropriate fungicides (e.g., copper-based or mancozeb) during early infection
• Avoid excessive nitrogen to reduce plant vulnerability
• Improve spacing and pruning to enhance air circulation
• Ensure proper drainage and avoid overhead irrigation
• Use disease-resistant varieties when available
